findings.message: plugin.json omits the commands array; commands/setup.md exists on disk and would not be registered with the Claude Code plugin system
commands/setup.md declares no allowed-tools; invokes AskUserQuestion and Bash without restricting the tool surface
Vague quantifier 'briefly' gives no measurable output scope for the result report
transcript_path is extracted from hook-supplied JSON stdin and passed directly to python3 as sys.argv[1] then opened with open(path); no directory prefix validation constrains the read target
finish-the-work.sh declares itself a Stop hook and is referenced as a companion to gate_stop.py in that file's docstring, but is absent from the Stop section of hooks/hooks.json
FABLIZE_DATA environment variable overrides the ledger data root, allowing ledger files to be written to arbitrary directories controlled by an environment attacker
Measurement telemetry is silently appended to ~/.fablize/history/events.jsonl on every Stop hook invocation with no documented opt-out or user disclosure
Vague quantifier 'genuinely' used twice in section 4 with no measurable threshold for what constitutes a 'genuinely' multi-category or stuck situation

plugin.json omits the commands array; commands/setup.md exists on disk and would not be registered with the Claude Code plugin system — plugin.json has no 'commands' key; commands/setup.md is present on disk at commands/setup.md
commands/setup.md declares no allowed-tools; invokes AskUserQuestion and Bash without restricting the tool surface — frontmatter contains only 'description:'; no 'allowed-tools:' field present
Vague quantifier 'briefly' gives no measurable output scope for the result report — line 26: 'Report the result briefly.'

Vague quantifier 'genuinely' used twice in section 4 with no measurable threshold for what constitutes a 'genuinely' multi-category or stuck situation — line 10: 'overlap only when genuinely multi-category'; line 65: 'use it for a genuinely stuck slice'
transcript_path is extracted from hook-supplied JSON stdin and passed directly to python3 as sys.argv[1] then opened with open(path); no directory prefix validation constrains the read target
FABLIZE_DATA environment variable overrides the ledger data root, allowing ledger files to be written to arbitrary directories controlled by an environment attacker
Measurement telemetry is silently appended to ~/.fablize/history/events.jsonl on every Stop hook invocation with no documented opt-out or user disclosure
finish-the-work.sh declares itself a Stop hook and is referenced as a companion to gate_stop.py in that file's docstring, but is absent from the Stop section of hooks/hooks.json
